hits.content: The URLValidator test suite loads URLs from tests/validators/valid_urls.txt (expected valid) and tests/validators/invalid_urls.txt (expected invalid) for dynamic test generation.
The fix adds `if username is None or password is None: return` after the username extraction from kwargs on line 42-43 of django/contrib/auth/backends.py.
The Django admin JSONField displays Unicode characters (e.g., Chinese) as ASCII escape sequences (\uXXXX) because `json.dumps()` defaults to `ensure_ascii=True`.
The bug: Django username validators (both ASCII and Unicode) accepted usernames ending with a newline because `$` in the original regex `r'^[\w.@+-]+$'` matched before a trailing `\n`.

Valid URL test cases added to valid_urls.txt with percent-encoded special characters in auth: http://user%40name@example.com, http://user:[REDACTED:Basic Auth Credentials]@example.com, http://user%2Fname@example.com.

query: PR 10097 URLValidator username password test cases requirements

hits.content: The fix adds `if username is None or password is None: return` after the username extraction from kwargs on line 42-43 of django/contrib/auth/backends.py.
The bug was introduced in commit bbe6fbb8768e8fb1aecb96d51c049d7ceaf802d3 and addressed in PR #14315.
The URLValidator test suite loads URLs from tests/validators/valid_urls.txt (expected valid) and tests/validators/invalid_urls.txt (expected invalid) for dynamic test generation.
The `test_negative` test in `tests/utils_tests/test_dateparse.py` covers negative duration cases: '-4 15:30', '-172800', '-15:30', '-1:15:30', and '-30.1'.
The bug: Django username validators (both ASCII and Unicode) accepted usernames ending with a newline because `$` in the original regex `r'^[\w.@+-]+$'` matched before a trailing `\n`.

Valid URL test cases added to valid_urls.txt with percent-encoded special characters in auth: http://user%40name@example.com, http://user:[REDACTED:Basic Auth Credentials]@example.com, http://user%2Fname@example.com.
The test test_crash_password_does_not_leak in tests/dbshell/test_postgresql.py had the same `if env:` bug pattern, which was fixed to `if env is not None:`.
Tests for PostgreSQL dbshell SSL options are in tests/dbshell/test_postgresql.py and verify each SSL parameter (sslcert, sslkey, sslmode, sslrootcert) individually and all together with password.
In Django's MySQL backend `get_connection_params`, the deprecated kwargs `'db'` and `'passwd'` must be replaced with `'database'` and `'password'` respectively when connecting to mysqlclient.
MySQL DatabaseClient.settings_to_cmd_args_env initializes env = None and only sets it to a dict when a password is present, correctly signaling 'no env overrides' via None.
